title: peltopus plicatus
side_effects: Peltopus plicatus can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant can lead to liver damage, diarrhea, and stomach cramps. It may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and antacids, causing adverse reactions. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can also cause respiratory problems, such as shortness of breath and wheezing.
